item.title: Iran’s Sanctions-Busting Crypto Ambitions Grow on Toll Payments
item.description: As a fragile US-Iran ceasefire takes shape, Tehran has signaled that payments in digital currency should form part of any toll system for vessels passing through the Strait of Hormuz, the chokepoint through which around a fifth of the world’s oil normally flows. The logic is clear: tokens cannot be easily confiscated under sanctions.

item.title: UBS Won’t Release Nazi Accounts Settlement Files Sought by Investigator After Court Setback
item.description: UBS Group AG says it won’t hand over a stash of privileged documents for a probe into Credit Suisse’s handling of Nazi-linked accounts after failing to win assurances that doing so wouldn’t expose it to new financial claims.

item.title: Why US-Iran Ceasefire Hasn’t Led to Hormuz Reopening
item.description: Maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz — a vital route for exports of oil, natural gas and other commodities from the Persian Gulf — remains severely constrained by Iran, even after a US-Iran ceasefire took effect on April 8.

item.title: Glencore and Mercuria Agree to Buy More LNG From Commonwealth
item.description: Glencore Plc. and Mercuria Energy Trading SA agreed to increase liquefied natural gas purchases under 20-year deals with an export terminal Kimmeridge Energy Management Co. is developing in Louisiana, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Blackstone Unit Lends $226 Million for US Outdoor Storage Lots
item.description: A unit of Blackstone Inc. provided a $226 million loan for a group of industrial outdoor storage facilities concentrated in the US Sunbelt.

item.title: Venezuelan Cops Block March as Pay Pledge Fails to Calm Workers
item.description: Venezuelan security forces blocked an anti-government march from reaching the presidential palace on Thursday, after a vague pledge to raise wages failed to calm workers angered by soaring prices.

item.title: Carney’s Rival Says He Must Help Homeowners in Indigenous Case
item.description: The political debate over a court ruling recognizing Indigenous land rights in the Vancouver area escalated on Thursday after Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre urged the prime minister to intervene.

item.title: NLB Joins Raiffeisen in Bidding Battle for Balkans Lender Addiko
item.description: Nova Ljubljanska banka d.d. said it plans to announce a purchase offer for shares in Balkans consumer lending specialist Addiko Bank AG, countering a similar proposal announced less than a day ago by Raiffeisen Bank International AG.

item.title: Nigeria’s Dollar Reserves Dwindle as Central Bank Defends Naira
item.description: Nigeria‘s naira has stayed largely stable through the market volatility sparked by the Iran war, but that came at a cost to the nation’s foreign-exchange reserves.

item.title: Mercuria Trader Who Built Huge LME Aluminum Bets Has Left
item.description: Sonny McNess, the veteran trader who helped build such massive aluminum positions at Mercuria Energy Group that the London Metal Exchange was forced to adjust its rules, has left the trading house.

item.title: Banxico’s Dissenters Warn About Price Pressures Due to Iran War
item.description: Mexico’s central bank’s decision to cut interest rates last week faced strong opposition from two of its five board members, who raised concerns about lingering inflationary pressures from the war in Iran.

item.title: Brazil Weighs Raising Ethanol Blend in Boost to Consumers, Mills
item.description: The world’s second-largest ethanol producer Brazil is considering raising the amount of ethanol in gasoline, in a move that would aid consumers facing higher fuel prices while also boosting demand for sugar-cane mills struggling with a biofuel supply glut.

item.title: FBI Breach Exposes Secret Investigative Records to Intruders
item.description: Prior cyber-espionage campaigns resulted in the theft of sealed records from US courts.

item.title: Ex-First Brands Officer Says He Was Kept in the Dark About Fraud
item.description: First Brands Group’s former chief strategy officer asked a judge to dismiss a civil lawsuit that blames him in part for the auto-parts supplier’s collapse, arguing that founder Patrick James and his brother, Edward, concealed the alleged fraud that brought down the company.

item.title: Oil Trading Costs Surge as ICE Hikes Crude, Diesel Margins
item.description: Intercontinental Exchange Inc. will boost the margins traders have to post for its Brent crude and European diesel futures contracts as the war in Iran sends volatility surging across commodity markets.

item.title: CloudHQ Seeks to Raise $1.4 Billion From Data-Center ABS Deal
item.description: Data-center operator CloudHQ plans to raise $1.4 billion through US asset-backed securities backed by a pair of Virginia facilities leased to undisclosed hyperscalers.

item.title: Wheat Drops to One-Month Low as USDA Boosts US, World Supplies
item.description: America’s wheat supplies are expanding as imports rise even as farmers plant less of the staple grain, according to the US Department of Agriculture’s latest monthly outlook.

item.title: Dawson Plans New Credit Fund Months After $7.7 Billion Close
item.description: Dawson Partners plans to hit the fundraising trail for its next flagship vehicle after the private credit manager closed a prior iteration in October with about $7.7 billion.

item.title: Iran War Erodes Exxon, Chevron Profits Even as Oil Prices Soar
item.description: The surge in oil and natural gas prices from the Iran war might seem like good news for the world’s biggest energy companies, but disruptions to production in the region, shipping blockades and hedging losses make the reality far more complicated.

item.title: Apollo’s Cornell, Client Coverage Head, Leaves After Four Years
item.description: Dennis Cornell, the head of client coverage for the Americas at Apollo Global Management, has left the firm, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: CoreWeave Bet Powers 7.4% Gain at $66 Billion Ontario Pension Fund
item.description: Investment Management Corp. of Ontario earned 7.4% last year thanks to strong equity gains and a roughly C$1 billion ($723 million) return from trimming its position in artificial intelligence cloud provider CoreWeave Inc.

item.title: Top Canadian Grocers Downgraded as Scotiabank Flags Increased Competition
item.description: Canadian grocers could be facing tougher times, according to analysts at Scotiabank, who have dimmed their outlook for the sector in light of increasing competition.

item.title: LA Metro Debt Sale Helps Pay for Subway Serving Olympics, UCLA
item.description: The Los Angeles County Metropolitan Transportation Authority sold about $900 million of debt this week to refund older bonds and finance transit projects, including an extension of a subway line that will serve UCLA and the future Olympic village for the 2028 Summer Games in the city.

item.title: Colombia Forces Pension Funds to Cut Assets Held Overseas to 30%
item.description: Colombia will force pension funds to invest more in the local economy by capping at 30% the proportion of assets they can hold overseas.

item.title: IMF Moves Toward Resuming Venezuela Ties With Survey of Members
item.description: The International Monetary Fund is distributing a survey to members asking about their relations with Venezuela, according to people familiar with the matter, a key step for the institution to potentially resume engagement with the oil-producing South American nation for the first time in decades.

item.title: Carlyle Caps Private Credit Redemptions After Investors Seek 16%
item.description: A $7 billion private credit fund managed by Carlyle Group Inc. capped redemptions after investors asked to pull 15.7% of the shares in the first quarter.

item.title: Software Slumps Again With Growth Hopes ‘Dashed on the Rocks’
item.description: Shares of software companies fell on Thursday, in the latest example of the group slumping amid concerns about disruption from artificial intelligence services.

item.title: Stocks Are Grinding Lower and Gapping Higher Under Trump
item.description: The market moves triggered by President Donald Trump’s foreign policy pronouncements are helping to shake up a long-established pattern in equities.

item.title: What to Know About the EU’s New Digital Border Checks
item.description: The European Union’s new digital border control system is set to be fully implemented from April 10, changing how the majority of member states handle the arrival of hundreds of millions of people every year.

item.title: Meta, Amazon, X.AI Pressed by Key Senator on Child Exploitation
item.description: A top Senate Republican is launching an inquiry into Meta Platforms Inc., Amazon.com Inc., X.AI Corp., and five other major tech companies over concerns with their reporting on suspected cases of online child sexual exploitation.
